part.text:
# Working Copy as Commit

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), the working copy is always a permanent commit. Any file change is automatically recorded in the current commit when you run any `jj` command. This eliminates the need for a separate staging area and makes it easy to track changes.

## Requirements
1. Initialize a new Git-compatible jj repository in the `/home/user/repo` directory.
2. Configure your identity for the repository (e.g., `jj config set --user user.name "Test User"` and `jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"`).
3. Create a file named `hello.txt` containing the text `Hello jj`.
4. Create a new empty working copy on top of your current changes (leaving the changes to `hello.txt` in the previous commit).

## Constraints
- The repository must be located at `/home/user/repo`.
- The file `hello.txt` must be committed in the parent of the current working copy (`@-`).
- The current working copy (`@`) must be empty.

# Working Copy as Commit

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), the working copy is always a permanent commit. Any file change is automatically recorded in the current commit when you run any `jj` command. This eliminates the need for a separate staging area and makes it easy to track changes.

## Requirements
1. Initialize a new Git-compatible jj repository in the `/home/user/repo` directory.
2. Configure your identity for the repository (e.g., `jj config set --user user.name "Test User"` and `jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"`).
3. Create a file named `hello.txt` containing the text `Hello jj`.
4. Create a new empty working copy on top of your current changes (leaving the changes to `hello.txt` in the previous commit).

## Constraints
- The repository must be located at `/home/user/repo`.
- The file `hello.txt` must be committed in the parent of the current working copy (`@-`).
- The current working copy (`@`) must be empty.

Initialized repo in "."
Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj config set --user user.name "Test User" && jj config set --user user.email "
cd /home/user/repo && jj config set --user user.name "Test User" && jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"
cd /home/user/repo && jj config set --user user.name "Test User" && jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"
cd /home/user/repo && jj config set --user user.name "Test User" && jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"

Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"
Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && echo "Hello jj" > hello.txt && jj new
cd /home/user/repo && echo "Hello jj" > hello.txt && jj new && jj log
cd /home/user/repo && echo "Hello jj" > hello.txt && jj new && jj log
cd /home/user/repo && echo "Hello jj" > hello.txt && jj new && jj log

output.output:
@  kwymvqks test@example.com 2026-04-22 05:04:02 faef7f1f
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  ylruxuyk test@example.com 2026-04-22 05:04:02 7c9371a0
│  (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
Working copy  (@) now at: kwymvqks faef7f1f (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : ylruxuyk 7c9371a0 (no description set)

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj status
cd /home/user/repo && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj status
cd /home/user/repo && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj status

output.output:
Added regular file hello.txt:
        1: Hello jj
---
The working copy has no changes.
Working copy  (@) : kwymvqks faef7f1f (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-): ylruxuyk 7c9371a0 (no description set)

Initialized a Git-compatible jj repository at `/home/user/repo` and configured the user identity (`Test User` / `test@example.com`).

Steps performed:
1. `jj git init` in `/home/user/repo`.
2. `jj config set --user user.name "Test User"` and `jj config set --user user.email "test@example.com"`.
3. Created `hello.txt` with content `Hello jj` (automatically recorded in the current working-copy commit).
4. Ran `jj new` to create a fresh empty working copy on top of the previous commit.

Resulting state:
- `@-` (parent): commit `7c9371a0` containing `hello.txt` with `Hello jj`.
- `@` (working copy): empty commit `faef7f1f` with no changes.

Verification:
- `jj diff -r @-` shows `hello.txt` was added with content `Hello jj`
